values.title: Welcome to NovaCMS
values.hero_title: The Open Source CMS for the Modern Web
values.hero_description: NovaCMS is a headless content management system built for developers who demand performance, extensibility, and freedom. Ship faster with a CMS that gets out of your way.
values.cta_description: Get started in minutes with our CLI installer, comprehensive documentation, and a thriving community of developers ready to help.

values.title: GraphQL API
values.body:
NovaCMS ships with a fully-featured GraphQL API out of the box. Query exactly the data you need with zero over-fetching, complete with real-time subscriptions, batched queries, and automatic schema generation from your content models.

Key Capabilities

  • Auto-generated schema from content types
  • Real-time subscriptions via WebSockets
  • Query complexity analysis and rate limiting
  • Built-in playground for schema exploration

image.alt: GraphQL API code on a dark terminal

values.title: Plugin Architecture
values.body:
Extend NovaCMS with a robust plugin system that lets you add custom fields, workflows, and integrations without modifying core code. Over 200 community plugins are available in the marketplace.

Plugin Features

  • Hook-based lifecycle events
  • Custom field type registration
  • Admin UI extensions
  • Hot-reloading in development

image.alt: Circuit board representing modular plugin architecture

values.title: Edge Caching & CDN
values.body:
Built-in edge caching delivers content in under 50ms worldwide. NovaCMS integrates with all major CDN providers and includes intelligent cache invalidation that keeps your content fresh without sacrificing speed.

Performance Highlights

  • Sub-50ms global response times
  • Automatic cache invalidation on content updates
  • Stale-while-revalidate support
  • Edge-side includes for partial caching

image.alt: Server room with glowing network connections

values.title: Flexible Authentication
values.body:
Support for OAuth 2.0, API keys, JWT tokens, and custom authentication providers. NovaCMS lets you secure your API however your team prefers, with granular role-based access control.

Auth Options

  • OAuth 2.0 with PKCE flow
  • JWT with refresh tokens
  • API key management
  • RBAC with custom roles and permissions

values.title: Elena Vasquez
values.body: Elena is the creator and lead maintainer of NovaCMS. She started the project in 2021 while working as a senior engineer at a digital agency, frustrated by the limitations of existing headless CMS options. Today she works full-time on NovaCMS, supported by the project's sponsors.

values.title: Kai Tanaka
values.body: Kai maintains the GraphQL engine and API layer of NovaCMS. With a background in distributed systems at major tech companies, Kai brings deep expertise in building high-performance APIs that scale to millions of requests per day.

values.title: Amara Osei
values.body: Amara leads the documentation and developer experience efforts for NovaCMS. She has rewritten the project's documentation from scratch, created the interactive tutorial system, and manages the community Discord server with over 8,000 members.

values.title: NovaCMS v3.0.0 - Aurora
values.body:
Breaking Changes

  • Minimum Node.js version bumped to 20 LTS
  • Legacy REST API removed in favor of GraphQL
  • Config file format changed to YAML

New Features

  • Real-time collaboration for content editing
  • AI-powered content suggestions
  • Built-in image optimization pipeline
  • Multi-tenant workspace support

Improvements

  • 50% reduction in cold start time
  • Redesigned admin dashboard
  • Improved TypeScript type generation

values.title: NovaCMS v2.12.0
values.body:
New Features

  • Webhook management UI
  • Content scheduling with timezone support
  • Bulk import/export via CLI

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed memory leak in long-running preview sessions
  • Corrected pagination cursor behavior on filtered queries
  • Resolved race condition in concurrent content updates

values.title: NovaCMS v2.11.3
values.body:
Bug Fixes

  • Patched XSS vulnerability in rich text field sanitization
  • Fixed Docker image build failure on ARM64 architecture
  • Corrected OAuth token refresh timing issue

Security

This release addresses CVE-2025-12345, a moderate-severity cross-site scripting vulnerability. All users on v2.11.x are encouraged to upgrade immediately.

values.title: About NovaCMS
values.body:
NovaCMS is an open source headless content management system licensed under the MIT License. The project was founded in 2021 with a simple goal: build the CMS that developers actually want to use.

Our Mission

We believe content management should be developer-friendly, blazing fast, and completely free from vendor lock-in. NovaCMS gives teams full ownership of their content infrastructure while providing the developer experience they deserve.

Governance

NovaCMS is governed by a Technical Steering Committee (TSC) of five elected maintainers. Major architectural decisions are made through an open RFC process, and all discussions happen in public on GitHub.

Sponsors

NovaCMS is sustained by the generous support of our sponsors, including corporate backers and individual contributors on GitHub Sponsors and Open Collective.

values.title: Documentation
values.body:
Getting Started

The fastest way to get started with NovaCMS is our CLI installer. Run the following command to create a new project:

npx create-novacms my-project

Core Concepts

  • Content Types - Define your content models with fields, validations, and relationships
  • API Layer - Query content through the auto-generated GraphQL API
  • Plugins - Extend functionality with community or custom plugins
  • Webhooks - Trigger external services on content events

Community Resources

  • Discord Server - 8,000+ members
  • GitHub Discussions - Q&A and feature requests
  • YouTube Channel - Video tutorials and live streams

values.title: Contributing Guide
values.body:
How to Contribute

NovaCMS welcomes contributions from developers of all experience levels. Whether you're fixing a typo in the docs or building a major feature, your help makes the project better for everyone.

Types of Contributions

  • Code - Bug fixes, features, and performance improvements
  • Documentation - Guides, tutorials, and API reference improvements
  • Community - Answering questions, reviewing PRs, and mentoring newcomers
  • Design - UI/UX improvements to the admin dashboard

Development Setup

Fork the repository, clone your fork, and run npm install followed by npm run dev. The project uses a monorepo structure managed with Turborepo.
